subject: Science
overall_learning_objectives: Analyze how habitats support different organisms
Evaluate interactions between biotic and abiotic components
Investigate population and community relationships in ecosystems
Interpret complex food chains, food webs, and trophic levels
Examine the role of decomposers in nutrient recycling
Assess the consequences of disturbances in ecosystems
Propose conservation strategies to maintain ecological balance

lesson_plan.title: Habitat Investigation
teaching_strategy: Case examples of forest and pond ecosystems
student_activities: Analyze how loss of forest cover affects wildlife movement
Compare two habitats and explain why organisms differ

lesson_plan.title: Biotic–Abiotic Interaction Analysis
topics_covered: Effects of environmental conditions on organisms
student_activities: Create a chart explaining interactions between living and non-living components
Explain how sunlight, water, and soil affect plant growth

lesson_plan.title: Population and Community Dynamics
student_activities: Survey a small ecosystem area and estimate population of organisms
Discuss how resource competition affects populations

lesson_plan.title: Ecosystem Interaction Model
student_activities: Design a simple ecosystem model using diagrams
Explain relationships between organisms and environment

lesson_plan.title: Food Chains, Food Webs, and Energy Flow
teaching_strategy: Case study of grassland food chain
student_activities: Construct a food web with at least six organisms
Predict what happens if one organism disappears

lesson_plan.title: Decomposition and Nutrient Cycling
student_activities: Observe decomposition of organic matter like leaves
Explain how decomposers maintain ecological balance

lesson_plan.title: Human Impact and Ecosystem Sustainability
student_activities: Analyze a real example of ecosystem disturbance

teaching_aids: Videos or images of natural ecosystems
teacher_tip: Encourage students to analyze real environmental issues such as deforestation, pollution, or biodiversity loss to understand how ecosystem balance can be disturbed and restored.
